concerns.text: FR-005 and US-3 explicitly require the real-world validation dataset to have 'at least 3 predictors'. Task T030 only specifies subsampling to N < 50. There is no task to verify or enforce the predictor count constraint, risking a degenerate regression model that violates the spec.

FR-005 and US-3 require applying the 'selected best-performing method' from the simulation. Task T031 assumes this selection has happened ('Run selected best method') but no task exists to actually identify/select the best method based on the simulation results (FR-004/US-2) before this step. The execution flow is broken.

SC-004 mandates completing 200 runs within 6 hours. Task T036 is a manual verification step ('Run full simulation... to verify'). The plan lacks an automated task or CI gate to enforce this performance constraint, conflicting with the Constitution's Reproducibility principle (FR-030) which requires fresh runs to validate results automatically.

FR-004 requires calculating coverage across 200 replications. Task T027 filters out runs with R-hat > 1.05. There is no task to ensure the *remaining* valid replications meet the statistical power requirements (e.g., >190 valid runs) implied by SC-001/SC-004. If many chains fail, the study may lack power, but no task addresses this contingency.

FR-006 requires flagging datasets with VIF > 10. Task T014 implements the calculation, but there is no explicit task to persist the VIF flag/metadata in the `data/simulated/` artifacts. Without this persistence, downstream tasks (like T027) cannot filter based on realized collinearity, breaking the data flow.
